title: noronhia broomeana var. cordemoyana
intro: Noronhia broomeana var. cordemoyana is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the ironwood tree. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of iriflophenone chalcone, a unique compound that exhibits antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Noronhia broomeana var. cordemoyana include wound healing, skin infections treatment, and potentially reducing the severity of certain diseases. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant involve making teas, infusions, or extracts from its leaves and bark, which can be used to treat various health issues.
